subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral + independent (IMF, World Bank, Freedom House, RSF, HRW, FIDH, Carnegie, Jamestown) + international press (RFE/RL, Eurasianet); state media EXCLUDED on governance.

An entrenched personalist autocracy - President Rahmon (in power since 1992) is engineering a dynastic handover to his son Rustam Emomali, with a 2024-2025 cadre purge clearing the path ahead of the 2027 election.

Standout drags: the world's most remittance-dependent economy (~45% of GDP, almost all from Russia, exposed to a post-Crocus migrant crackdown), GBAO/Pamiri repression, escalating Afghan-border (ISKP) attacks, and a Rogun mega-dam the World Bank won't yet fund.

economic.notes: Decent headline macro but the world's most remittance-dependent base, with a large Rogun contingent liability.

fiscal_state.notes: Public debt fell to ~25% of GDP (end-2024) on strong revenue and growth, with an IMF PCI on track, but Rogun is a large contingent liability. (IMF 2025 Art IV; 3rd PCI Review)

monetary_independence.notes: A managed somoni with periodic devaluations - policy is tightly state-directed and remittance/ruble-driven. (IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The somoni is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~2.8% y/y (Sept 2025), within the central bank's target band. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

banking_stability.notes: Rapid 2025 asset growth, but a legacy of bank failures, state direction, and a thin, low-trust system. (IMF; State Dept ICS 2025)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A narrow base (remittances, aluminum, gold) with reserves ~8 months of imports; Rogun electricity export is the long-bet but unfinanced. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: A personalist autocracy with no judicial independence, pervasive corruption, and jailed/exiled journalists.

rule_of_law.notes: A personalist autocracy with no judicial independence, pervasive corruption, and prosecutions weaponized against dissent and the Pamiri minority. (Freedom House 2025; HRW 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: IMF-engaged economic data (a PCI program) but opaque political/social reporting. (IMF; V-Dem)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Clientelist and purge-prone (a 2024-25 Interior Ministry purge), with weak delivery in a poor, rural state. (Eurasianet; Jamestown)

press_freedom.notes: RSF ~153-155/180 - journalists jailed or exiled, with pervasive self-censorship. (RSF 2025/2026; CPJ)

political_social.notes: Repression-bought near-term calm against a contested dynastic succession and Pamiri persecution.

political_stability.notes: Repression buys near-term calm, but an unfinished father-to-son dynastic succession makes the medium term a significant fracture risk. (Jamestown; Eurasianet)

civil_liberties.notes: Among the world's least free - assembly, religion, and dissent crushed, with transnational repression abroad. (Freedom House 2025; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Not a destination, with severe treatment of internal minorities/exiles; mirror risk: its own ~1m migrants face a xenophobic crackdown in Russia. (HRW 2025; RFE/RL)

social_cohesion.notes: Systematic GBAO/Pamiri repression - ~40 killed and 200+ detained after the 2022 crackdown, with deaths of Pamiri prisoners reported in 2025. (HRW 2025; FIDH)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing, but mass labour emigration and high poverty/dependency undercut the dividend. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: Russia-dependent with growing Chinese inroads, and escalating Afghan-border attacks.

alliance_reliability.notes: A CSTO member structurally dependent on Russia (remittances, a base, security guarantees) with growing Chinese inroads - not autonomous. (Carnegie; Jamestown)

conflict_involvement.notes: Escalating cross-border ISKP attacks from Afghanistan (drone strikes killed workers in Nov 2024 and Nov 2025) with internal GBAO unrest a second front. (Al Jazeera; Jamestown)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not directly sanctioned but acutely exposed to Russia spillover (sanctions, ruble, migrant expulsions), with a managed account. (IMF; HRW)

physical_practical.notes: Glacier-loss-driven winter energy crises, the region's poorest health system, and a major dam tail risk.

climate.notes: Severe glacier loss and falling run-off drive recurring winter energy crises (the Nurek reservoir down ~2.5m in a year). (Al-Monitor; Times of Central Asia)

healthcare.notes: The region's poorest health system - low spend, heavy reliance on remittance-funded and out-of-pocket care. (WHO; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: ~95% hydro-dependent power with chronic winter shortages; Rogun could double generation but is years and ~$6.4bn from completion. (IEA; World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate everyday crime but elevated political-violence and border-security risk. (soft/indirect)

disaster_insurance.notes: High seismic, GLOF/landslide, and flood hazard with near-zero penetration, plus a major dam-safety tail risk at Rogun. (seismic-hazard literature; World Bank)

summary: An entrenched personalist autocracy where Rahmon, in power since 1992, is steering a dynastic handover to his son Rustam Emomali, against a backdrop of severe repression - near-floor civil liberties, jailed/exiled journalists, and systematic erasure of the Pamiri (GBAO) minority. Its economy is the world's most remittance-dependent (~45% of GDP, almost all from Russia), an exposure made acute by Russia's post-Crocus crackdown on the ~1m Tajik migrants who sustain it. The headline macro picture is decent (low debt ~25% of GDP, ~2.8% inflation, an on-track IMF program), and the Rogun mega-dam is the long-term development bet, but Rogun needs ~$6.4bn the World Bank won't yet fund. Escalating ISKP attacks from Afghanistan, chronic hydro-driven winter energy crises, and a contested succession make the medium-term outlook clearly negative.
